blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: About how most of the things I've been most afraid of in my life turned out to be less about the thing itself and more about what I thought would happen to me when I had to live with it.
She was looking past his shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: The thing itself was usually survivable. It was the anticipation that was actually hard.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I think I've been doing that. The anticipation thing. With the thing I want to tell you. I've been living inside an anticipated version of it for so long that I've started confusing the anticipation with the thing itself.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: And I think I'm ready to stop doing that.
He moved back slightly — just enough to see her face.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Not tonight. Tomorrow. I want to tell you tomorrow. Properly, in the right way.
He looked at her for a long moment. The candlelight made the room feel like its own country — sealed from the outside, with its own rules.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'm going to receive it right. Whatever it is. I've been deciding that for a while.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I know. That's part of why I'm ready.
He drew her back in, and she let him, and they moved together in the dim room while the music played at its low and steady volume, and neither of them said anything for a while.
